domain: Sand dollar (Echinarachnius, Mellita) pentameric radial symmetry — adult body plan 5-fold radial with 5 ambulacra petalloid plates equally spaced around central oral region.
specific_mechanism: Adult develops 5-fold radial symmetry from bilateral larva; 5 ambulacra are independent functional units (gas exchange, podia locomotion) arranged with C5 cyclic-group symmetry around central axis.
llm_application: PENTA-RADIAL-AGG: partition each attention head's head_dim into 5 orthogonal 'petal' subspaces of dim head_dim/5; compute attention independently in each petal (Q,K,V projected into each petal); aggregate the 5 petal-outputs via a learned C5-equivariant radial recomposition (uses irreducible representation of cyclic group C5 to enforce 5-fold rotational symmetry of aggregated output). Imposes pentameric inductive bias.
novelty_claim: Standard multi-head attention partitions head_dim without group-theoretic symmetry constraints. Hadamard butterfly mixing (2603.08343) is SO(2)^n-like, not cyclic-group. MAHA (2512.14925) is depth-multiscale, not radial. Group-equivariant networks exist in vision (e.g., for image rotations) but C5-equivariant within-head attention subspace decomposition for LLMs has not been published.
